sprint: Final Engineering Sprint - Enterprise Production Readiness
scope: Production-quality UX polish and application hardening only. Existing architecture, APIs, authentication, permissions, reports, dashboards, object engine, Composite API, and performance caches were preserved.

skeletonLoading: Added shared skeleton treatment for existing state-box, report busy, dashboard busy, and loading row elements.
Preserves existing DOM structure and avoids layout shifts.
toastManager: Added centralized queue-based toast manager with success, error, warning, and info variants.
Prevents overlapping by limiting active notifications and queues remaining messages.
Bridges existing toast(message, type, duration) calls without changing call sites.
unsavedChangesProtection: Tracks dirty forms and modal-like containers automatically.
Warns before browser close/refresh, link navigation, close buttons, and browser back when edits are present.
Does not interrupt navigation when no values have changed.
friendlyErrors: Maps technical network/session/server/permission failures to user-friendly messages.
Global error and unhandled promise handlers route failures through the toast manager.
emptyStates: Adds consistent polish hooks for existing empty and error states across CRM, reports, dashboards, and search results.
topLoadingBar: Only becomes visible after approximately 300 ms.
offlineDetection: Displays lightweight notifications and preserves current UI state.
accessibility: Adds dialog roles to dynamically observed modal containers.
Adds aria-labels to icon-only buttons when title text exists.

productionHardeningCompleted: Added request ID propagation on frontend fetch requests.
Added security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, and HTTPS-only HSTS.
Added global frontend error boundary and unhandled rejection handling.
Converted remaining intentional server console.log calls to console.info.
Added production readiness assets to build hash inputs for cache busting.
preservedBehavior: No existing API response schemas changed.
No authentication, session, RBAC, FLS, sharing, reports, dashboards, layout, related list, object engine, Composite API, or Salesforce integration logic changed.
No new backend storage or infrastructure introduced.
No additional API calls on initial page load except existing application behavior; command/search requests remain user-initiated.
remainingTechnicalDebt: Some page-specific loading overlays still originate from legacy page scripts; the readiness layer polishes them non-invasively rather than rewriting each page flow.
Full automated browser regression coverage would be the next quality investment, but was not introduced to avoid changing the engineering architecture.
